query: US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows late June 2026 and early July 2026, weekly trend from Farside Investors and SoSoValue. Are net outflows continuing into the week of June 29-July 3 2026? What is the Bitcoin price trend in late June 2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's the summary of findings on US spot Bitcoin ETF net flows and Bitcoin price trends for late June/early July 2026:

**ETF Flows — Week of June 29–July 3, 2026:**
- **Yes, net outflows continued into the START of that week** — the first three sessions were all negative (Jun 29: −$243.5M, Jun 30: −$246.6M, Jul 1: −$283.8M per Farside/bitbo), totaling roughly −$750M.
- **But the streak broke mid-week**: July 2 flipped to net inflows (ending a 10-day, ~$2.7B outflow streak), and July 3 continued positive (+$169.9M). Inflows extended through July 2–7 (~$510M).

**June 2026 context:** The worst outflow month on record — ~$4.06B net redemptions (SoSoValue), IBIT-driven (~73–75%), with the final week of June alone at ~$1.79B.

**Bitcoin price trend, late June 2026:** Firmly downward — from ~$66K mid-June to a low of ~$58,559 on June 30 (coinciding with peak outflows), an ~11–12% drawdown. It then recovered to ~$62–64K in early July as flows turned positive, reportedly helped by a weak June jobs report easing Fed rate-hike fears.

One caveat: minor figure discrepancies exist between Farside snapshots (data revisions), but the directional trend is consistent across all sources. Full structured data with daily figures and sources has been recorded.

key_facts: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded ~$4.06 billion net outflows in June 2026 (SoSoValue), the largest monthly redemption since Jan 2024 launch, surpassing prior record ~$3.56B (Feb 2025).
BlackRock's IBIT accounted for ~73-75% (~$3.3B) of June 2026 outflows.
The final week of June 2026 saw ~$1.79 billion in redemptions.
Week of June 29-July 3, 2026: first three sessions were net outflows (Jun 29: -$243.5M, Jun 30: -$246.6M, Jul 1: -$283.8M per Farside/bitbo).
July 2, 2026 broke a 10-day outflow streak with net inflows (+$32.2M per bitbo, +$223.5M per KuCoin/Farside), largest single-day intake in ~2 months.
July 3, 2026 continued inflows (+$169.9M); July 2-7 totaled ~$510M inflows.
Bitcoin price fell in late June 2026, bottoming at ~$58,559 close on June 30 (period low).
Bitcoin recovered to ~$62,544 by July 3 and ~$63,995 by July 6, 2026.
The July 2 reversal was reportedly aided by a weaker-than-expected June jobs report easing Fed rate-hike fears.
BTC 3-month total return through July 9, 2026 was -12.3% with ~30% annualized volatility.
